HART Fundamentals

1. What is the HART protocol?
“HART” is an acronym for Highway Addressable Remote Transducer. The HART Protocol superimposes digital communication signals at a low level on top of a 4-20mA signal using the Frequency Shift Keying (FSK) standard. The HART protocol is used worldwide to send and receive digital information on analog current loops (4-20 mA) between field instruments and distributed control systems.[1]

2. How HART protocol work?
Using the same 4-20mA wiring used for analog communications in Channel 1 from the Rheonics SME, a digital signal is encoded with a technique known as Frequency Shift Keying(FSK). Data from the digital signal includes PV, status, diagnostics, and additional measurements or calculations.
key_facts: HART stands for Highway Addressable Remote Transducer.
The HART Protocol superimposes digital communication signals at a low level on top of a 4-20mA signal.
HART uses the Frequency Shift Keying (FSK) standard for digital communication.
The HART protocol is utilized globally for transmitting and receiving digital information over analog current loops (4-20 mA) between field instruments and distributed control systems.
The Rheonics SME employs 4-20mA wiring for analog communications, and a digital signal is encoded using Frequency Shift Keying (FSK).
Data transmitted via the HART digital signal includes Process Variable (PV), status, diagnostics, and additional measurements or calculations.

4. HART Commands
According to the HART Protocol, all communication takes place through a request (or command) from the host communication device during normal operation. For example, the host may be a PLC, distributed control system, or a PC-based asset management system.

Figure 3. HART command list.

HART Command List (Pyramid):
- Non-public: For manufacturer's use for maintenance
- Wireless HART: Command designed specifically for WirelessHART devices.
- Device Specific: Device Specific commands represent functions that are unique to each field device.
- Common Practice: Common Practice commands provide functions implemented by many, but not necessarily all, HART Communication devices.
- Universal: All devices using the HART Protocol must recognize and support the universal commands.

5. What configuration do I need in a Rheonics HART device?
5.1. Short address
Rheonics sensor by default is set to a polling address 0, this can be edited at any time by the Rheonics Support time when applications with multidrop devices are needed.
key_facts: The HART Protocol enables communication via requests or commands from a host device, such as a PLC, distributed control system, or PC-based asset management system.
HART commands are classified into Universal, Common Practice, Device Specific, Wireless HART, and Non-public categories.
All devices using the HART Protocol are required to recognize and support universal commands.
Rheonics sensors are factory-set to a default polling address of 0.
The polling address of a Rheonics sensor can be modified by Rheonics Support for multidrop device applications.
diagram_labels: Figure 2. HART Topology with Rheonics Sensor
Command designed specifically for WirelessHART devices.
Device Specific commands represent functions that are unique to each field device.
Common Practice commands provide functions implemented by many, but not necessarily all, HART Communication devices.
All devices using the HART Protocol must recognize and support the universal commands.

chunks.page_summary: This page details HART communication devices, specifically focusing on Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) and providing examples of HART-compatible analog input cards from Siemens and Allen-Bradley for industrial automation.
chunks.extracted_text:
5.2. HART communication devices
,Common devices that are used for HART communication are PLC, DCS, HART modem and HART communication.

5.2.1. PLC
A Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) is a digital computer that uses inputs and outputs to automate industrial processes. HART communication requires special hardware, which is not available in every analog card used in PLCs. Therefore, it is important to select the right analog card when the application requires HART communication.
Some examples of HART analog input cards:

5.2.1.1. Siemens/ET 200SP HA
The Siemens SIMATIC ET 200SP HA Interface Module IM 155-6 PN HA supports HART communication.

Figure 4. Analog card with HART-compatible SIMATIC ET 200SP HA[2]

5.2.1.2. Allen-Bradley/ControlLogix HART I/O Modules
These modules are designed to maximize system performance by combining real-time HART data with standard analog data. They're part of the Allen-Bradley/Rockwell Automation brand.
key_facts: Common devices for HART communication include PLCs, DCS, and HART modems.
A Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) is a digital computer designed to automate industrial processes through inputs and outputs.
HART communication in PLCs necessitates special hardware, specifically the appropriate analog card, which is not universally available in all analog cards.
The Siemens SIMATIC ET 200SP HA Interface Module IM 155-6 PN HA provides support for HART communication.
Allen-Bradley/ControlLogix HART I/O Modules are engineered to enhance system performance by integrating real-time HART data with conventional analog data.
Allen-Bradley/ControlLogix modules are components of the Allen-Bradley/Rockwell Automation brand.
diagram_labels: Figure 4. Analog card with HART-compatible SIMATIC ET 200SP HA[2]

5.2.1.3. Emerson/RX3i Analog Input module, HART
This HART module has 16 channels single-ended and 8 channels differential. The channels are configurable for voltage, current, and current/HART.

Figure 6. HART analog input module IC695ALG626 - RX3i[4]

5.2.1.4. Phoenix Contact/AXL P AI8 HART 1F - Analog module
The analog input I/O module with HART capabilities collects analog input and HART data from the field I/O terminals and makes this information available to the bus coupler through the AxioLine P local bus.
key_facts: The Emerson/RX3i HART module has 16 single-ended channels and 8 differential channels.
The channels of the Emerson/RX3i module are configurable for voltage, current, and current/HART.
The Phoenix Contact/AXL P AI8 HART 1F - Analog module collects analog input and HART data from field I/O terminals.
The Phoenix Contact/AXL module makes collected information available to the bus coupler through the AxioLine P local bus.

chunks.page_summary: This page introduces the benefits of Distributed Control Systems (DCS) for HART devices, illustrating an Analog Input S/R HART module (AI845) and detailing the Honeywell C300 Controller as a core DCS component.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 9. Analog Input S/R HART 8 ch AI845 [7]

5.2.2. DCS
One of the key advantages of using DCS for HART devices is the ability to access detailed information about the devices. DCS can read and interpret the digital data transmitted by HART devices, providing valuable insights into their health, status, and diagnostics. This information can be used for predictive maintenance, troubleshooting, and optimizing process performance.

Some examples of DCS modules:

5.2.2.1. Honeywell|C300
The Honeywell C300 Controller is part of Honeywell's Experion LX and Experion PKS distributed Control Systems (DCS). This controller is the core unit for control and data acquisition in these systems, and it's designed to enhance process control performance and reliability.
key_facts: One key advantage of using DCS for HART devices is the ability to access detailed information about the devices.
DCS can read and interpret digital data transmitted by HART devices, offering insights into device health, status, and diagnostics.
Information from DCS and HART devices can be utilized for predictive maintenance, troubleshooting, and optimizing process performance.
The Honeywell C300 Controller is a core unit for control and data acquisition within Honeywell's Experion LX and Experion PKS Distributed Control Systems.
The Honeywell C300 Controller is designed to enhance process control performance and reliability.

chunks.page_summary: This page details how Distributed Control Systems (DCS) like ABB System 800xA and Rockwell PlantPAx, along with HART field communicators, enable communication with and management of HART-enabled devices.
product_names: Rockwell Automation's PlantPAx Distributed Control System (DCS)
chunks.extracted_text:
The System 800xA can be used to communicate with HART-enabled devices. This means that System 800xA can collect, configure, calibrate, and diagnose HART devices. It does this through HART-capable I/O modules, which can communicate with HART devices and bring their data back to the System 800xA.

Figure 12. DCS ABB System 800xA[10]

5.2.2.4. Rockwell automation|PlantPAx

Rockwell Automation's PlantPAx Distributed Control System (DCS) supports the use of HART (Highway Addressable Remote Transducer) protocol through specific I/O modules. These modules can communicate with HART-enabled devices, allowing for enhanced device management and diagnostics.

PlantPAx
Distributed Control System

Figure 13. DCS Rockwell automation PlantPAx[11]

5.2.3. Field Communicator

The HART communicator is ideal for monitoring or calibrating multiple instruments or for monitoring instruments in hazardous areas. The HART communicator does not require physical contact with the instrument, so it can be used in places where physical access is limited or hazardous. It also provides the ability to monitor or calibrate multiple instruments, which can be especially helpful in industrial or hazardous environments.
key_facts: The System 800xA can communicate with HART-enabled devices to collect, configure, calibrate, and diagnose them through HART-capable I/O modules.

Rockwell Automation's PlantPAx Distributed Control System (DCS) supports the HART (Highway Addressable Remote Transducer) protocol via specific I/O modules for enhanced device management and diagnostics.
HART communicators are suitable for monitoring or calibrating multiple instruments in hazardous areas or where physical access is limited, as they do not require physical contact with the instrument.

product_names: HART modem Commubox FXA195 USB/HART modem
Rheonics inline viscosity and density meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 14. Yokogawa YHC5150X Portable Hart Communicator(Left)[12] and Emerson AMS Trex Device Communicator (Right) [13]

5.2.4. HART Modem
The HART modem is a signal modulation/demodulation device that exchanges information efficiently over a serial link. It is in compliance with the HART protocol. The HART modem allows users to send and receive data between two devices over a serial link. It is a simple and reliable communication solution and can be used with a variety of devices.

Figure 15. HART modem Commubox FXA195 USB/HART modem [14]

6. Does Rheonics inline viscosity and density meter support HART?
key_facts: HART modems are signal modulation/demodulation devices designed for efficient information exchange over a serial link.
HART modems comply with the HART protocol.
HART modems allow users to send and receive data between two devices over a serial link.
HART modems provide a simple and reliable communication solution for a variety of devices.

chunks.page_summary: This page details how Rheonics supports the HART communication protocol for its sensors, outlines its partnership with the FieldComm Group, and describes the available technical support and resources for HART integration.
chunks.extracted_text:
Yes, all Rheonics SME sensor electronics support the HART communication protocol. It can be ordered prior to the shipment or can be enabled remotely. To order the HART communication protocol on Rheonics sensors, check for the C8-HART option on the RFQ page for the specific device you are ordering, eg. inline viscometer SRV or inline density meter SRD. More information on how to order the HART communication protocol can be found in this support article: Getting Started with HART communication (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412295)

7. How does Rheonics support HART integration and development?

FieldComm Group is an organization that develops standards for communication and integration between devices and systems in the petrochemical industry. They provide specifications, test procedures, and other documentation for digital communication technologies. They also provide training and support services to help companies implement these technologies. HART partnership between FieldComm Group and Rheonics (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412192-hart-partnership-between-fieldcomm-group-and-rheonics)

Rheonics provides detailed user manuals and technical support for integration. Our HART-compatible sensors can be easily integrated into any HART network thanks to all the guidelines and steps provided in our support portal. In addition, our experienced engineering team can assist with installation and configuration to ensure successful integration into your HART network in a timely manner. Visit our Support portal for more information on HART Protocol: HART (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000284399)

8. REFERENCES
key_facts: All Rheonics SME sensor electronics support the HART communication protocol.
The HART communication protocol can be ordered prior to shipment or enabled remotely.
To order the HART communication protocol on Rheonics sensors, customers should check for the C8-HART option on the RFQ page for the specific device.
FieldComm Group is an organization that develops standards for communication and integration between devices and systems in the petrochemical industry.
FieldComm Group provides specifications, test procedures, documentation, training, and support services for digital communication technologies.
Rheonics is a member of the FieldComm Group, as indicated in Figure 16.
Rheonics provides detailed user manuals and technical support for HART integration.
Rheonics' HART-compatible sensors can be easily integrated into any HART network using guidelines and steps from their support portal.
Rheonics' experienced engineering team can assist with the installation and configuration of HART network integration.
Further information on HART communication, the partnership with FieldComm Group, and HART Protocol support is available via provided Rheonics support articles and portals.

key_facts: The page provides a list of 14 external resources and product pages related to HART (Highway Addressable Remote Transducer) communication technology.
The resources cover various industrial automation components and systems, including I/O modules, signal conditioners, distributed control systems, and HART communicators.
Manufacturers such as Siemens, Rockwell Automation, Emerson, Phoenix Contact, Schneider Electric, ABB, Honeywell, Yokogawa, and Endress+Hauser are featured for their HART-compatible products.
